content.full_text:
July 14, 1988


Eva S. Bates
Treasurer, Friends of Harriet Ross
1333 Jones Street (603)
San Francisco, CA 94109

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-88-248

Dear Ms. Bates:

You have requested advice concerning your campaign disclosure
obligations under provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").1/

QUESTION

May a candidate for supervisor use the name "Friends of ..."
for her campaign committee?

CONCLUSION

The Act does not require nor prohibit the use of any
particular name for a candidate's campaign committee.

FACTS

Harriet Ross, candidate for supervisor in the City and County
of San Francisco, has a registered campaign committee which uses
"Friends of ..." as part of the committee name.


1/Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seg. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Eva S. Bates
Page 2

ANALYSIS

     When a candidate for public office forms a campaign committee
to receive contributions and to make expenditures on behalf of the
candidate, then a statement of organization must be filed with the
Secretary of State.  (Sections 82013(a) and 84101.)  The statement
of organization must contain the name of the committee.  (Section
84102(a).)  However, there are no provisions in the Act which
specify what name a candidate's committee shall use.  Therefore,
any name is permitted under the Act.

     San Francisco has adopted a campaign disclosure ordinance
which applies to elections for that jurisdiction.  (see Section
81013.)  "Friends" committees have been interpreted to have
special meaning under the requirements of the San Francisco
campaign disclosure ordinance.  The Commission does not interpret
the meaning of a local ordinance, so you should consult with
officials in San Francisco for information about their campaign
disclosure requirements.

     In summary, the name of a candidate's campaign committee is
not specified by the Act.  If a particular name, such as "Friends
of ...", has a special meaning under the provisions of a local
jurisdiction's campaign disclosure ordinance, officials in the
relevant jurisdiction should be contacted for interpretations of
their requirements.

     If you have further questions regarding this advice, please
contact me at (916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

                                        [illegible]
                              By: Bruce W. Robeck
                                  Political Reform Consultant

FRIENDS OF
HARRIET ROSS

JUN 29 2437PM 68
June 29, 1988

Dear Holden Justice Commission
228 V Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

b.ID.8801125

Dear Sue:

Harriet Ross is a candidate for
superior court in the Foothills. We female
supporters of Harriet Ross are asking for
the purpose of her election to the
office. In particular we regard their
friends of Harriet Ross my understanding their
Friends of. As a different consolidation,
Records. We paper able find under this
name for the obvious purpose of election
to the office. Should the name of the campaign
be changed?

Sincerely,

Ina N. Bates, Treasurer

1333 JONES STREET (603)
SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94109

(415) 775-3523

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


June 29, 1988


Eve S. Bates
Friends of Harriet Ross
1333 Jones Street (603)
San Francisco, CA  94109

Re:  88-248

Dear Ms. Bates:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on June 28, 1988 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission.  If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice.  If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to the information needed.  If your request is
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we
can.  (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs.
Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Jeanne Pritchard
Chief
Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division
